tasks.subject: Task 1: parseLabeledDivider pure parser
tasks.description:
**Goal:** A pure, ProseMirror-free function that classifies a paragraph's text as a centered or left labeled divider (or neither) and returns the character ranges of the hidden dash runs and the visible label.

**Files:**
- Create: `app/src/lib/editor/labeledDivider/parseLabeledDivider.ts`
- Test: `app/tests/unit/editor/parseLabeledDivider.test.ts`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- `-- label --` (2+ dashes each side) parses as `align: 'center'`.
- `label ---` (3+ trailing dashes, no leading dashes) parses as `align: 'left'`.
- Dashes-on-both-sides input resolves to `center` (centered checked first).
- A pure dash run (`---`, `-----`) returns `null`.
- A label that is all-dashes/whitespace, or (for `left`) starts with a dash, returns `null`.
- Returned ranges are contiguous and the label range matches the `label` string.

**Verify:** `cd app && npx vitest run tests/unit/editor/parseLabeledDivider.test.ts` -> all tests pass.

```json:metadata
{"files": ["app/src/lib/editor/labeledDivider/parseLabeledDivider.ts", "app/tests/unit/editor/parseLabeledDivider.test.ts"], "verifyCommand": "cd app && npx vitest run tests/unit/editor/parseLabeledDivider.test.ts", "acceptanceCriteria": ["centered -- label -- parses as center", "left label --- parses as left", "dashes both sides resolve to center", "pure dash runs return null", "all-dash/whitespace or leading-dash labels return null", "ranges contiguous and match label"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 2: labeledDividerPlugin ProseMirror plugin
tasks.description:
**Goal:** A decoration-only ProseMirror plugin that, for each top-level paragraph (skipping the title + subtitle lines), runs `parseLabeledDivider` and emits a node decoration (line + alignment class) plus inline decorations (hide dash runs, style label). **Files:** - Create: `app/src/lib/editor/labeledDivider/labeledDividerPlugin.ts` - Test: `app/tests/unit/editor/labeledDividerPlugin.test.ts` **Acceptance Criteria:** - A centered-divider paragraph at index >= 2 gets `

`. - A left-divider paragraph gets the `tomboy-labeled-divider--left` class. - The label is wrapped in `.tomboy-labeled-divider-label`; dash runs in `.tomboy-labeled-divider-mark`. - Paragraphs at index 0 and 1 (title/subtitle) are never decorated. - Plain paragraphs and pure `---` HR paragraphs are not decorated. - Decorations recompute when the document changes. **Verify:** `cd app && npx vitest run tests/unit/editor/labeledDividerPlugin.test.ts` -> all tests pass. ```json:metadata {"files": ["app/src/lib/editor/labeledDivider/labeledDividerPlugin.ts", "app/tests/unit/editor/labeledDividerPlugin.test.ts"], "verifyCommand": "cd app && npx vitest run tests/unit/editor/labeledDividerPlugin.test.ts", "acceptanceCriteria": ["centered divider gets --center node class", "left divider gets --left node class", "label in .tomboy-labeled-divider-label span, dashes in .tomboy-labeled-divider-mark", "index 0 and 1 never decorated", "plain paragraphs and pure --- HR not decorated", "decorations recompute on doc change"]} ```

tasks.subject: Task 3: Register plugin in editor and add divider CSS
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Wire `createLabeledDividerPlugin` into `TomboyEditor.svelte`'s extension list and add the CSS that paints the divider line and styles the label, so the feature is live in the running app.

**Files:**
- Modify: `app/src/lib/editor/TomboyEditor.svelte` (import near hrSplit import ~line 52-54; new `Extension.create` after the `tomboyHrSplit` block ~line 466; CSS after the `tomboy-hr-marker` rules, before `.tomboy-hr-split-active` ~line 1342)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- `npm run check` passes (no new type errors).
- Full test suite (`npm run test`) passes.
- In the dev server, typing `-- label --` renders a centered divider; typing `label ---` renders a left divider.
- The label stays directly editable; a real `---` HR still renders as a plain horizontal line.

**Verify:** `cd app && npm run check && npm run test` -> both succeed; then `npm run dev` and visually confirm both divider layouts (manual).

```json:metadata
{"files": ["app/src/lib/editor/TomboyEditor.svelte"], "verifyCommand": "cd app && npm run check && npm run test", "acceptanceCriteria": ["npm run check passes with no new type errors", "npm run test full suite passes", "-- label -- renders a centered divider in dev", "label --- renders a left divider in dev", "label stays editable and plain --- HR unchanged"]}
```
